title: Item Filter (Hopper)
description: Sorts one specific item type into a dedicated chest using a hopper with named/locked slots. The filter hopper sits below a main chest; five named items in the hopper slots lock each slot to that item type only. Non-matching items pass through to the output chest below.

steps.title: Input chest
steps.explanation: Place a chest at (0,3,0). This is where all mixed items enter. Items will flow downward through the hopper chain.

steps.title: Filter hopper
steps.explanation: Place a hopper at (0,2,0) pointing down (hopper's output faces down by default when placed under a chest). Fill all 5 hopper slots with the item you want to filter (e.g. 5 diamonds). This locks those slots — only diamonds can flow through, everything else is rejected.

steps.title: Filtered item chest
steps.explanation: Place a chest at (0,1,0) below the filter hopper. Matching items (diamonds in our example) land here. Non-matching items are not pulled through the filter hopper — they stay in the input chest.

steps.title: Overflow hopper
steps.explanation: Optionally add a second hopper at (0,2,0) pointed sideways into an overflow chest at (1,2,0). Any items that don't match the filter (remained in the input chest) can be routed sideways by a separate hopper beside the input chest. This gives you a complete two-output filter: filtered items go down, everything else goes sideways.
